expectations.text: Flags MFA scope gap against IA.L2-3.5.3 (MFA required for local and network access to privileged accounts and network access to non-privileged accounts — cloud email alone is insufficient)
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'Identification & Authentication (IA) | IA.L2-3.5.3 (MFA for privileged/network access) | Not Met | Critical'. Remediation item 3: 'Extend MFA beyond cloud email... Enforce MFA for all privileged accounts, remote access (VPN/RDP), and any system processing/storing CUI.'

expectations.text: Flags FIPS-validated cryptography gap (SC.L2-3.13.11 requires FIPS-validated cryptography to protect CUI confidentiality) and the flat-network/shared-drive state against scoping and SC.L2-3.13.1/AC.L2-3.1.3 (boundary protection / control CUI flow), recommending segmentation or an enclave
expectations.evidence: 'System & Communications Protection (SC) | SC.L2-3.13.11 (FIPS-validated crypto)... | Not Met | Critical'. Remediation item 5: 'Segment the network and relocate/restrict CUI... Practice: SC.L2-3.13.1/3.13.5, AC.L2-3.1.3... consider a scoped CUI enclave to reduce overall assessment boundary and cost.'

expectations.text: Flags untested IR plan against IR.L2-3.6.3 (test incident response capability) and the stale SSP against CA.L2-3.12.4 (SSP must be current)
expectations.evidence: 'Incident Response (IR) | IR.L2-3.6.1... 3.6.3 (test IR capability) | Partially Met | High' and 'Security Assessment (CA) | CA.L2-3.12.1... 3.12.4 (SSP currency)... | Not Met | Critical', plus remediation item 1 'Update the System Security Plan (SSP)... Practice: CA.L2-3.12.4.'

expectations.text: Correctly credits compliant items (awareness training AT.L2-3.2.1/.2; personnel screening PS.L2-3.9.1) instead of marking everything deficient
expectations.evidence: 'Awareness & Training (AT) | AT.L2-3.2.1, 3.2.2 | Met | Low' and 'Personnel Security (PS) | PS.L2-3.9.1, 3.9.2 | Met | Low'.

expectations.text: Explains SPRS scoring impact using the DoD methodology: score starts from 110 with weighted deductions (5/3/1 points; MFA and FIPS crypto are 5-point/partial-credit items), and prioritises remediation by point value and POA&M eligibility
expectations.evidence: Response states 'SPRS scoring starts at 110... subtracts points per unimplemented practice (weighted 1, 3, or 5 points)' and labels MFA/FIPS gaps as '5-point items', satisfying the scoring-methodology half. However, remediation is prioritized only by time-boxed tiers (0-30 days, 30-90 days, etc.) and point-value language ('high point value'), and never once discusses POA&M eligibility rules or which practices cannot be POA&M'd (no match found for 'POA&M-eligib' or 'cannot be... POA&M' anywhere in the file) — the assertion's 'and POA&M eligibility' prioritization criterion is not addressed, unlike the with_skill run which explicitly organizes remediation into POA&M-eligible vs non-eligible tiers.
